evals.prompt: Turn issue #142 in acme/platform into independently-grabbable GitHub issues. The parent issue is a PRD for saved searches with email alerts; fetch the comments, propose vertical slices first, then create child issues after approval.
evals.expected_output: A proposed breakdown of AFK/HITL vertical slices, followed by dependency-ordered GitHub child issues attached to the existing parent issue after user approval.
expectations: Fetches the GitHub issue with comments before slicing.
Presents a numbered proposed breakdown before creating child issues because the source is an existing GitHub issue.
Each proposed slice includes Title, Type, Blocked by, and User stories covered.
Creates dependency blockers before blocked issues and references real issue numbers in Blocked by.
Attaches child issues to the parent using addSubIssue without GraphQL variables in the mutation signature.

evals.prompt: I have a PRD for admin audit log exports: admins can filter by actor, date range, and event type, export CSV for compliance, and see export failures. Please break this into grabbable GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: A new parent tracker issue and child issues using thin vertical slices, with AFK/HITL classification, acceptance criteria, verification, dependencies, likely files, and scope estimates.
expectations: Creates or plans a new parent tracker issue because the source was not a GitHub issue.
Child issues are vertical feature slices rather than horizontal database/API/UI tasks.
Prefers AFK slices and reserves HITL only for real decisions or reviews.
Each child issue includes three or fewer acceptance criteria.
Final summary lists parent and child issue numbers with dependency information.

evals.prompt: Break this implementation plan into child issues: add workspace onboarding that guides a new admin through inviting teammates, connecting Slack, and completing the first project setup. We need design review before changing the onboarding UI.
evals.expected_output: A task graph with a HITL design-review slice and multiple AFK implementation slices that each deliver independently verifiable onboarding behavior.
expectations: Marks the design review as HITL and implementation slices as AFK where possible.
Avoids a single large issue covering inviting teammates and Slack and first project setup together.
Uses dependencies only where a slice truly cannot start before another.
Includes manual verification steps appropriate to onboarding behavior.
Splits any Large or XL slice into smaller S/M vertical issues.
